channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Pakistan deepens ties with Turkey and Egypt ahead of Middle East de-escalation talks
item.description: On Sunday, Deputy Prime Minister Ishaq Dar held separate meetings with the foreign ministers of Turkey and Egypt in Islamabad, where all sides reaffirmed their commitment to strengthening cooperation ...

item.title: Prince Andrew moves into Sandringham home with second-hand caravan for security staff
item.description: Prince Andrew, Duke of York, has reportedly moved from his former residence at Royal Lodge to a more modest property on the Sandringham Estate, marking a significant change in his circumstances. Once ...

item.title: Government calls on cooperatives to increase investment in the Co-operative Bank of Tanzania to secure majority ownership
item.description: The government has directed financially strong cooperative societies in Tanzania to double their minimum share contributions in the Co-operative Bank of Tanzania from five to ten percent by 30 April 2...

item.title: Drap denies recent essential drug price increase, says local production stable despite global tensions
item.description: The Drug Regulatory Authority of Pakistan (Drap) has denied reports suggesting that the prices of essential medicines in Pakistan have seen an increase. In an official statement, Drap clarified that t...

item.title: Tanzania Records 10 Percent Decline in Crime as Prison Numbers Fall
item.description: Crime in Tanzania has dropped by 10 percent, according to new statistics from the Tanzania Prisons Service, which show a notable decrease in the number of inmates across the nation. The data, presente...

item.title: TASAF’s PSSN programme strengthens poverty reduction without political influence
item.description: The Tanzania Social Action Fund (TASAF), through its Productive Social Safety Net (PSSN) programme, has continued to play a critical role in improving the welfare of poor and vulnerable households acr...

item.title: Royal Navy ship deployment, UK arrests in UAE, postal price rises, and other news highlights
item.description: Several news stories have emerged over the weekend, covering a wide range of topics. A man has been arrested in Derby after a car struck several people, with witnesses describing a scene of 'absolute ...

item.title: Small traders face barriers to access government-backed loans despite allocated billions
item.description: Small-scale traders in Kahama and across Tanzania are facing difficulties accessing government-backed, interest-free loans, even though billions of shillings have been set aside to support them. Accor...

item.title: Pakistan and Egypt reaffirm commitment to deepen bilateral cooperation ahead of Middle East quadrilateral talks
item.description: Pakistan and Egypt have reaffirmed their strong commitment to deepen cooperation across all areas of mutual interest following a meeting between their foreign ministers in Islamabad on Sunday, accordi...

item.title: Peshawar Zalmi chase record total to beat Rawalpindiz in PSL opener
item.description: Peshawar Zalmi began their HBL Pakistan Super League 11 campaign with an impressive five-wicket win over newcomers Rawalpindiz at Lahore’s Gaddafi Stadium. Chasing a tough target of 215, Zalmi’s batti...

item.title: Tanzanian researchers trial smart pillbox to boost TB treatment adherence and shorten recovery time
item.description: In Dar es Salaam, scientists from the Ifakara Health Institute are testing an electronic pillbox known as the Wisepill box to support tuberculosis (TB) patients in following their medication schedules...

item.title: North Korea Advances Missile Technology Amid Regional Concerns
item.description: North Korea has conducted what appears to be another missile test, sending ripples across the region and raising concerns about escalating tensions. While details are still emerging, analysts suggest ...

item.title: Sanwo-Olu’s media aide shares how police recover his stolen phone at APC convention
item.description: Wale Ajetunmobi, wey be Senior Special Assistant on Media to Lagos State Governor Babajide Sanwo-Olu, don share how hin iPhone 17 Pro Max waka comot for hin pocket during the All Progressives Congress...

item.title: The Geopolitical and Strategic Importance of the Spratly Islands in the South China Sea
item.description: The article by Diego González explores the complex territorial disputes surrounding the Spratly Islands, a group of tiny, mostly uninhabitable islets and reefs scattered across the South China Sea. De...

item.title: Global Inflation Concerns Rise Amidst Ongoing Conflicts
item.description: Recent global conflicts are significantly impacting worldwide economies, particularly through soaring inflation rates. The article discusses how these wars, along with related geopolitical tensions, a...

item.title: Canva reports growth in revenue but continues to post net losses
item.description: Recent filings by Canva Australia with the corporate regulator reveal that the design software company has continued to record net losses, despite achieving positive cash flow and strong revenue growt...

item.title: Experts urge humane solutions as rabies cases rise in Karachi
item.description: Another tragic death due to rabies has been reported in Karachi, bringing the city’s total fatalities this year to seven. The deceased was a 42-year-old rickshaw driver, the only earner for his family...

item.title: President Samia and Leaders Honour the Legacy of the Late Minister William Lukuvi
item.description: President Samia Suluhu Hassan led mourners in paying their final respects to the late Minister William Lukuvi, describing his passing as a great loss to Tanzania’s leadership at a time of global econo...

item.title: IMF Agreement with Pakistan Amid Rising Global Energy Turmoil
item.description: The latest staff-level agreement between the International Monetary Fund (IMF) and Pakistan has come at a most uncertain and volatile time. What under normal circumstances would have been taken as a r...

item.title: Pakistan Ports See Surge in Petroleum Handling Amidst Middle East Tensions
item.description: Yaar, things are getting a bit interesting at Pakistan's ports, especially Port Qasim. Because of Iran closing the Strait of Hormuz after those US-Israel attacks, shipping lines are diverting, and Por...
